codeReview.summary: The fix adequately addresses the prior failure by restoring the mission service manifest to the documented shared-backend contract while keeping the Redis healthcheck runnable in this environment. The reviewed commit no longer introduces worker-owned backend lifecycle behavior that could terminate the shared port-8001 runtime, so the feature’s baseline backend reachability assumptions remain aligned with mission boundaries.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill still leaves room for workers to interpret a runtime-baseline task as permission to add backend process-management logic, even when mission guidance requires reusing the existing backend on port 8001. The prior failed fix explicitly cited this ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend-worker SKILL.md step 4 says to verify contracts with curl on 8001, but does not remind workers to preserve mission ownership boundaries from AGENTS.md/environment.md. The failed prior review and the a557acbb handoff both describe this ambiguity around whether editing `.factory/services.yaml` to own backend start/stop is acceptable.

summary: Reviewed the prior failed review, feature metadata, fix handoff, transcript skeleton, current service manifest, and the fix commit diff. Result: pass. The fix commit is limited to the Redis healthcheck line in `.factory/services.yaml`; it removes the prior worker-managed backend start/stop change and leaves the manifest aligned with the mission rule to reuse the shared backend on port 8001, so the original scrutiny failure is adequately addressed.
